Name: 18_resume_applying_experience_job
Representative_Docs: ['i just accepted a job offer my first real job out of grad school yay given how difficult finding a job is especially during a global pandemic and lockdown i collected statistics in an excel spreadsheet throughout the process and i ve decided to do a postmortem statistical analysis and share it publicly here in the event that it might be helpful for others note that these numbers only represent my situation and perspective and may not be applicable to everyone overall numbers total jobs applied ghosted never responded rejected immediately coding test recorded interview phone screening in person zoom interview non initial rejection voluntarily declined offer accepted overall response rate overall rejection rate i ve also included some job board specific numbers amp x b some key takeaways job hunting just in general is a fuck networking with recruiters either through linkedin rd party agencies job fairs etc seems to be the best way to actually get a response and thereby get your foot in the door but it certainly doesn t guarantee an offer overall glassdoor and indeed had the best response rates but the sample sizes are greatly skewed in general i found indeed to be the best of the job boards at least since the pandemic began for some reason ziprecruiter has gone down the toilet in the past few months which is a shame because i got two internships through there before i also found handshake to be somewhat useful but this is only applicable to college students and recent graduates applying directly through the company website doesn t seem to be any better than using a job board in my experience linkedin is a total waste of time for job applications use it for connecting with recruiters otherwise don t bother using tailored r sum s actually works i didn t alter it for all job applications of course i just had for example one general r sum for software engineering jobs a machine learning data science specific one etc cover letters are not that important but it probably doesn t hurt apply to jobs that fit your skill level this can be hard as some asshole employers will list jobs that require years of experience and a ph d as entry level but try to sift through that bullshit applying to mid and senior level jobs that you are underqualified for will get you a lot of responses but they will all be rejections applying to jobs you are overqualified for will always get you ghosted possibly after a very very awkward interview i generally applied to jobs per day if you re applying to multiple locations cities more may be better i did not grind leetcode at all but i am not in the silicon valley area so again this may not apply to everyone the most i ever used leetcode was just occasionally to practice for coding assessments usually through hackerrank', 'i landed a systems engineering position as a mechanical engineer paying k in socal and i want to talk a little bit about what i think helped me land it and i guess show that it s not impossible to get one i had applied to jobs between june and september and i only landed interviews one of which turned into an offer as the title mentioned i had a and went to a non target school and had zero internship experience i honestly did not think i was going to get a job within a year of graduation but i did and this is what i think helped me just because school ended doesn t mean that your learning stops you have a huge opportunity to show that you are a self starter after you graduate rack up certificates and work on projects in things you are interested in i spent on coursera plus and it fucking hurt i did not really have much money saved up but i knew i had to do something to make myself competitive i started learning python sql and machine learning mostly because i was really interested in expanding my skillset and i have a deep fanboy appreciation for self driving car technology and wanted to get a foundational understanding of how that works and see if i could break into the automotive industry as a ml engineer i had finished some courses and threw them on my resume as well as some that were still in progress but i planned to complete i also was working with a team on a startup project and was able to put that on my resume and that ended up being a major point of conversation in the interview if i hadn t been self learning all summer i would not have been a competitive candidate nor would i have had anything to talk about during the interview as it s a primarily data science based position use your network i saw on linkedin that one of my classmates started at a new position at the beginning of summer and even though i saw the update a month late i messaged him and congratulated him i asked him about his role how he likes it and how he thinks he was able to demonstrate that he was a good fit for the role a few weeks later an operations manager at his job posted that he was looking to hire more new grads as they were looking to expand and my friend sent me the post i messaged the om and told him i was interested and sent my resume we set up an interview and two weeks later i found out i was selected i even mentioned my buddy in the interview and luckily the om knew him and loved the guy even if you aren t that close talk to the people in your network whether they re doing really well or in the same position as you prepare for the interview don t half ass it for an hour before the interview starts the second you have an interview scheduled all of your extra time should be spent preparing for it at least if it s important to you i come from a household where i m already set to make more than my parents combined so it was important to me to get this job to be able to help them and help myself what does preparing for the interview look like given that we are in a global pandemic most interviews are conducted via zoom or phone call and to me that makes it x easier i make a very organized document containing information about the interviewer the company the position i m applying for main points from my resume i want to highlight a scripted elevator pitch answers to every resume question i could find for this company online glassdoor and more general questions and questions i have for the interviewer throughout the interview i consistently referenced the document and it helped me answer tougher questions smoothly and that probably helped set me apart it probably took hours to put together and i spent a few hours reviewing it i also found out that i had another classmate that worked there so i was able to get the experiences of two separate people on the same interview this goes back to using your network as much as you can i wrote this in minutes while sitting on the toilet because i just saw a post about a student who was very upset in his job search and i just wanted to share my experience maybe there s something in here that can help somebody if there s anything y all would like me to elaborate on let me know and i ll try my best to help', 'applying to jobs is not at all the same as applying to a much smaller number of listings that have a much greater suitability for hiring a new grad i d guess at any given time the odds that any particular online job listing is really a rewording of some generic contract shop s desire to fill up their applicant db is about the best source of jobs for a recent hire is your school s career services office alumni network even if you ve been getting paid while in school which does seem to be the case from the resume image though you mention above about years of programming experience this is confusing if you told that on a phone screen i would be stumped there is high likelihood that the firms most interested in recent grad will be reached through the school s resources i have seen lots of resumes and i really do not want to sound harsh but this format is not helping me understand why i should consider you at all this might sound petty but honestly i do not care when you started college just tell me when you finished if i am relying on some janky scanning solution from hr it might report that your degree was earned in and then there is no work experience with relevant dates so it is going to make you look like a horrible slacker when i review resumes from new grads whether they are from schools i have great familiarity with or one i never heard of i want to see relevant info about specific courses concentrations they had in cs i have hired a few grads from northwestern so i know the options they have for the large enterprise scale work i am involved with it is very helpful to hire those with experience in databases distributed systems security and software development i have respect for grads with coursework in things like computer vision graphics game design and other areas but i am not going to hire them for the business related solutions i need them to work on if they got any honors or a gpa that is above i want to see that on the resume because it gives me and my team an idea of how success driven they might be if it ain t there and its a good school i won t view that as negative we ll just have to figure out other ways to decide what sorts of work habits the applicant has btw this is the excellent range of courses that northwestern grads have to choose from i mention this because this is why i like to see some mention of where the focus was if your school had similar path you can see why details matter i like to see resumes from grads who ve done any kind of internship i have an issue with grads that do not specifically know that internships are not the same as professional experience which though i grok that most internships pay pretty well they are not the same in terms of evaluation potential to verify employment which is also one of those areas that hr makes a nightmare when i review what the intern did i want to see good correlation with what i am asking for in the job posting if the intern had experience with tools that are not part of my team s workflow i have no problem with them being mentioned but i really need evidence that the applicant also has a sense of learning things from that internshi p about building enterprise systems that might be conveyed by mentions of methodologies like agile tools like git jira or trello specific platforms ranging from aws azure gcp or oss from linux ios or android and anything that else that helps fill the picture of how the internship translates to what my team works with i do not directly lead any ui designers but my team does work with them as well as having some members who are quite proficient with ux issues that said in my organization that skill is not as highly sought in new hires as it is among folks filling specialized roles i mention this because i know i have a tendency to scan resumes from top to bottom when something is first on the list that tends to shape how i view the rest of the items i believe others tend toward this bias as well point being if your goal is not primarily to be involved in the visual aspects of application development it makes sense to re order your resume perhaps tailoring it for each position you apply for final resume critique as others have said the bar chart is not helping in fact though i am not the type to throw away any resume for typographical errors i know many people who do and the length of the bar for javascript is the same as for php though one is label advanced and the other intermediate annoying confusing python is a great skill and i have interviewed recent grads that honestly do have very good skills same for java generally that means they ve studied the languages and implemented projects using somewhat advanced features you have the evidence of that with the german grammar checker that sounds quite interesting as well as lietner flashcards i had to google lietner and it seems to have the potential for plenty of good interview question that me and my team would ask you about sequence sort scoring if you implemented the whole system and not just the gui please make that clear those both sound like great ways to showcase a whole lot of skills you absolutely should link to your repos my team would be upset if they did not get a chance to see your code as to the travel blog i can see your reluctance to just throw it open with your potentially very casual pictures and such but since you do mention that you had wire frames mockup it really behooves you to showcase those skills for my team i would put that after the other two project but again that is a reason to tailor resume for each posting i am sure there are some firms that would be eager to review those wire frames mockups and have someone with your skills on the appropriate team i sincerely hope this points will help you improve your resume get more appropriate interviews and embark on what could be very promising career']

february am by alex barker and patricia nilsson in london gt a quiet revolution is sweeping the bn academic publishing market and its main operator elsevier partly driven by an unlikely group of rebels cash strapped librarians gt when florida state university cancelled its big deal contract for all elsevier s journals last march to save money the publisher warned it would backfire and cost the library m extra in pay per view fees gt but even to the surprise of gale etschmaier dean of fsu s library the charges after eight months were actually less than elsevier has not come back to us about the big deal she said noting it had made up a quarter of her content budget before the terms were changed gt gt mutinous librarians such as ms etschmaier remain in a minority but are one of a host of pressures bearing down on the subscription business of elsevier the year old publisher that produces titles including the world s oldest medical journal the lancet gt the company is facing a profound shift in the way it does business as customers reject traditional charging structures gt open access publishing the move to break down paywalls and make scientific research free to read is upending the funding model for journals at the behest of regulators and some big research funders while online tools and the illicit russian pirate site sci hub are taking readers gt even donald trump s administration in december began consulting on an executive order to liberate publicly funded research according to people briefed on the process gt column chart of articles showing growth in number of journal articles published by elsevier at risk is the profit powerhouse of elsevier s parent company uk listed relx europe s biggest media company by market capitalisation which reports its annual results on thursday the academic division s bn revenues are just a third of company turnover and grew at about per cent in but the chunky margins roughly per cent mean it accounted for per cent of relx s operating profit gt so far elsevier has defied doomsayers who have claimed it is a structurally compromised business gt but its willingness to experiment has increased markedly since kumsal bayazit an istanbul born former management consultant took over as chief executive last year admitting elsevier s transition to open access was too slow she is now stepping up one of the big evolutions of the company s history gt several stalled negotiations over access to journals have been unblocked including with sweden s bibsam consortium of higher education and research institutions in november one bibsam member said negotiations were dead until ms bayazit arrived and elsevier s team received new directives from above we understand the game it was giving them bad publicity that we were managing without access to elsevier journals the consortium member said gt rivals such as german owned springer nature and informa s taylor francis were quicker than elsevier to experiment with different pay to publish models the funding mechanism for open access papers more than three quarters of springer nature authors in britain sweden and the netherlands now publish via free to read journals gt while elsevier supported open access for an additional article processing fee it held out against demands from research institutions to bundle publishing rights with journal subscriptions for roughly the same overall contract price gt a graphic with no description jean claude burgelman the eu s open access envoy has noted real u turns at big publishers of late recent draft deals notably elsevier s with dutch institutions in december show how commercial value is moving from selling the end product the journal to other services such as data tools offered to scientists during the research cycle gt but only bad news like cancelling the deals seems to induce change he said at a conference last month gt elsevier s previous foot dragging may be no surprise given the blessed commercial model of academic publishing typically scholars have submitted their research for free to publishers who use volunteers to vet it before selling the edited journals back at a premium price to the universities that footed the bill for the original scholarship gt while prominent funders such as the wellcome trust and the bill amp melinda gates foundation have backed moves to open access publishing some academics have worried it could prevent their work appearing in the most prestigious journals an important factor in career assessment gt one of the old system s weak points was the university libraries elsevier executives note that their content budgets simply failed to keep up with the to per cent increases in research funding or the even bigger increases in elsevier s workload and output it received m submissions last year for articles tensions resulting from these issues have eroded trust between scholarly publishers and the research community that we serve said ms bayazit last month she even offered an extraordinary apology to librarians still angry over double digit price rises in the s and s gt ivy anderson co chair of the university of california s publishing negotiations team which cancelled its m contract with elsevier in march said at the time that academics were getting fed up with high prices and paywall journals they re standing up and saying we are willing to bear the inconvenience of not having journal access gt line chart of share price and index rebased showing relx s strong run ms etschmaier s big deal contract at fsu was by no means the largest of the negotiated by elsevier nor the hardest its disputes with the university of california and another with a consortium of german institutions are more significant gt but fsu s ability to cope is a vivid example of how the market is changing and eroding elsevier s pricing power a study by our research a non profit supporter of open access publishing found per cent of all journal articles in were outside of the traditional paywall gt elsevier also tested a new approach in a draft deal with dutch institutions which potentially provides unlimited publishing rights and covers its analytics services and data tools for the first time gt this potentially creates a path for the company to recreate its indispensable position as a journal publisher in the business of providing academic research from idea generation and funding to data collection and publication gt thomas singlehurst an analyst at citi who recently upgraded relx s shares to a buy said the big question was whether elsevier s conciliatory tone would extend to accepting cut price offers on its journals to encourage use of tools and services in short we think it might be necessary to concede defeat on some battles to win the bigger longer term war he said']

Name: 20_calculus_algebra_linear_linear algebra
Representative_Docs: ['ml is heavy in probabilty calculus and linear algebra and other non trivial topics here s a link to a companion site for a book called mathematics for machine learning coursera has a course specialization that covers linear algebra multivariate calculus and something called principal component analysis pca they look like the focus on the bare minimun needed to get into ml not saying it s easy i studied single varible calculus in university but not linear algebra la la doesn t look that bad the calculus course seems to only cover differentiation and focuses on types of functions at that after that they move on to multivariate calculus finding the derivative is actually not that hard the problem with calculus courses is that they spend the first month showing the hard way of doing things only half way through do they show the rules for finding derivatives which makes it super easy i can t speak for the last course but a note on the site says it s harder than the fist two and that if you make it past the first week you should manage', 'gt i come more from a stat background but where exactly do you need all these cs skills like data structures traditional algorithms memory management pointers etc when doing ml depends on what you mean by ml most of ml software use all of that in varying layers of the stack all ml are algorithms implemented through software that runs on hardware take libsvm for example which is used by toolkits in python java r matlab correctness and efficiency of implementation is important if you want people to use your implmentation gt seems like you can implement all the ml algs without much cs knowlege beyond the very basics of loops ifelse functions etc existing sort functions and numpy simplifies it amp x b naive implementations are usually straightforward in high level languages like python or matlab which is why it is a common learning exercise in many ml courses to implment from scratch the advantage of numpy and scikit learn is that they are well tested documented and provide a base to build more domain specific applications such as mne neuroimaging and scikit image computer vision amp x b gt even oop isn t needed to do ml algs as you can implement them from scratch in purely functional languages like r julia matlab in these languges vector matrix algebra even exists by default and in julia its not even calling c parallel computing i guess is a place where traditional cs comes in but the r libraries make it easy to treat it like a black box and make it easy oop is a paradigm of programming just like functional programming or parallel programming all of which are used in many common ml libraries such as pytorch and xgboost i m not sure why think oop is very related to traditional cs unless you conflate cs with software engineering just because a lot of work went into building the easy to use interface what i assume you mean by black box doesn t mean that work didn t entail many topics in cs as well as software engineering amp x b gt so where does actual cs come into play in this field im just trying to get a sense for why ml is done mostly in cs departments again what do you mean by actual cs if by ml you mean applied statistics many departments use ml such as economics physics geography biology etc if you re asking why ml is usually taught by cs departments is because many cs as well as statistics and applied math professors publish research in ml conferences and have capacity to teach a course on it i m not sure what other department you would expect to teach ml theory amp x b gt and why do people ask leetcode questions in ml interviews when that stuff has nothing to do with the statistical alg side of ml why not ask the statistical algs and numerical computing this is company specific and many companies do also ask questions on statistics probability and numerical computing competitive programming style e g leetcode questions are common for many positions that involve programming amp x b gt is ml in some sense the subfield with the least real programming i wonder if this is what makes some people who like programming get bored of all the math involved and not developing actual things and they move on to actual software engineering some very theoretic areas of cs such as complexity theory do not involve much if any programming some parts of ml such as statistical learning theory are more math than code if you enjoy programming the good news is that most ml jobs involve more programming than math the closer you get to industry and application the higher code math ratio evan as a ml scientist in a academic research lab i code everyday and work on projects that incorporate many software engineering practices version control containerization code reviews ci cd as well as more ml specific programming such as implementing loss functions scheduling training jobs and processing large amounts of high dimensional data', 'most machine learning is a mix of statistics linear algebra and calculus statistics for modelling the problems and then calculus and linear algebra for taking the model and turning into a problem that we can then solve numerically with a computer']

Name: 23_discord_interested_help_group
Representative_Docs: ['server invite discord gg ks brqc i have seen the immense support people have shown in this server and many like this and it just inspires me or any other aspiring programmer to keep striding in this field i am a year old programmer and game developer with interests in ai and ml who came here months ago to find a coding partner with whom i could seriously learn programming with i ended with up much more a community of programming newbies as well as trained professionals who are very experienced and earn a living through this field months later we just reached members and i have learned more about computers and programming than i had ever learned before and also went on to learn the basics of a few more languages from the motivation of my server colleagues this server is dedicated to programming primarily it was started for collaborating on projects but it turned out to be so much more since there were so many people joining if you want to do a project you should visit the team building channel share your idea and gather as many people as you think you will need for any further management i e create channels assign roles etc to get going dm one of the mods or the admin if you want to join a project ask the others what they are working on currently if they need help and what specific skill you might offer the main purpose of this server currently is offering a community to share code and try to help each other when we can there are a lot of channels dedicated to different programming languages and there is also a help channel to help you out on a particular query if someone is already getting help in one of those channels please consider using another one if possible and when you re done with all the programming come over to the offtopic channels for some casual talks we would be happy to have you here', 'edit i am no longer accepting additional requests for on tutoring a few people dm d me after i was full asking if i could do a small class instead i am open to this possibility and will continue to accept dms only for a small class on discord of maybe people of a similar skill level if enough people are interested i will do it please do not dm me asking for individual tutoring all of the spots are filled amp x b hi everyone so as a result of the coronavirus i ve noticed a lot of people are finding the time to get into programming recently and i think that s awesome if you are one of those people but are having trouble or don t really know where to start i would like to offer my assistance amp x b what i m offering free lessons once a week or twice a week going through a curriculum i wrote that covers most of the programming fundamentals in python java or c these are the only languages i m going to be offering but you can pick whichever you d like to do i can also recommend one if you don t know which to pick i ll probably take to people on a first come first serve basis i would prefer to teach people that have none or very little experience currently as that is what the curriculum that i have is targeted towards i am in est and would probably want to do it in the afternoon or evening est amp x b what you need a computer that you can program on a mic discord or maybe skype if you really hate discord amp x b my credentials i ve been programming for about years now i ve tutored a few times in the past online and in person i m still in my undergrad but have year of industry experience and years of freelance experience i mostly focus my time towards ai data science and machine learning in both my personal projects and work amp x b why i m doing this so i m not gonna lie mainly it s because i m bored af lol my classes are all online now cause of the virus and i have so much free time but on a serious note i know that when i was getting into programming it was really hard to grasp certain concepts i often wished i had someone to help me understand them better or just get more clarification than a youtube video or online tutorial can provide so now that i m where i m at now i wanna be able to be that person for other people so that they don t get discouraged and quit and instead get to really enjoy all programming has to offer plus tutoring is a lot of fun for me amp x b what s the catch the only thing that i guess you could call a catch is in the future if i end up applying for a tutoring position one day i may want to use you as a reference if everything goes well this is free all the way through amp x b that s pretty much it though i hope to be able to help you guys out', 'you may have seen u ar s post about a similar discord server for interns affected by the recent cancellations most of the comments on that post expressed concern that a group so unorganized won t get much done as an alternative i present to you projects for programmers pfp how does pfp manage its projects experience and language framework roles when new users join they will be given the opportunity to select their discord roles experience roles describes their overall programming and industry knowledge high school student someone who is beginning to learn programming concepts lower undergraduate someone who has worked on multiple projects upper undergraduate someone who has had multiple internships or had some industry experience industry expert someone who is knowledgeable about current industry standards best practices etc industry experts are asked to change their name to show what they are an expert or have in depth experience in along with how many years they have been in the industry ex johndoe gt johndoe full stack developer years or john doe machine learning years although these roles seem odd compared to beginner intermediate advanced they match the overall reddit populous to some extent offer a somewhat more objective experience level language frameworks roles the languages and frameworks users are comfortable enough with to work on a project in those languages these range from the classic java c and html to game engines like unity and unreal the list is currently incomplete so anyone who would like a language role that s not included can send a request both of these types of roles allow others to quickly identify which languages someone knows and their overall programming experience new project applications users can fill out a google form to submit a project to be placed on a list text channel after a quick verification that this project has a decent initial plan description and a few other things a voice and text channel is set up within the discord the user s who submitted the project is are invited to the github organization and made into project managers a github repository is setup along with a corresponding github team the discord channels and github repository team is given a project id which is simply a digit number the project and provided information from the google form is placed in the project list other users can ctrl f to search for types of projects they would like to work on the new project managers will be asked to set up their project boards with a general milestone road map github org structure the discord moderators create the repositories for each project the general naming structure id projectname ex example project each repository is setup with default labels to organize issues and pull requests from seantrane github teams since project managers are largely responsible for their own success and maintenance they can form teams and split the workload with each other bringing in others as they see fit rules this group has a relatively small set of rules to help ensure that things do not get too chaotic including no illegal content don t post say racist sexist or homophobic comments keep project discussions within their own text channels and a few more moderation we have a moderation team that will regularly check that each project is organized and provide a good description and or road map for others to quickly get started ensure that users behave and not wreak havoc address questions and requests if you would like to join this discord this server is brand new so any comments and suggestions are welcome also feel free to criticize it and tell me why it sucks please ask any questions you have i will be here to answer questions all day tl dr pfp is an organized and structured discord server for starting and contributing to open source projects this is an alternative server to u ar s discord group if you are a programmer looking for a new project to work on especially after being impacted by recent events in some way you are welcome to join us']

Name: 24_interview_interviewer_questions_interviews
Representative_Docs: ['edit warning this post is really long so i was called at pm sharp for my penn interview it was conducted with what i m guessing to be a young late s early s man he did his masters in social work at penn the formal part of the interview questioning was rather short about minutes he asked me what i ve accomplished what i hope to accomplish in the future and at penn what i like about penn and if there s anything i didn t say in my application during this time i talked about basically everything penn because i love penn and was very enthusiastic about it here are the answers i had to each of the questions in no particular order what i like about penn i talked first about wharton as i applied there and their leadership pathway part of the curriculum and how i think it would be very useful for me i then touched briefly about how the pre professional environment aligns with my interests i also talked about how penn s large community has a lot of clubs opportunities and other points of accessibility for me i d prefer be part of a very large campus with a lot of things to do and be a part of i also got excited about having a formal economics education which i haven t been able to have what i ve accomplished i talked about my work in building up some of the clubs i ve been a part of at my high school including my co founding and leadership roles in academic decathlon model un and especially speech and debate as well as a community service club i made with a friend at our school i emphasized how i wanted to take part in these activities so i set out to make it happen so i and others could enjoy them i touched on how i wasn t involved in freshman year since i was getting my bearings and then how i started to get deeply involved after that i talked about my main hobby stock trading and investing and what i ve done with that and how i want to pursue that line of work deeper what i hope to accomplish my topic of conversation here was the democratization of the investment banking industry as someone passionate in this field of work i stated my hope to gain the training and skills necessary to take up a line of work in the field and make it more accessible to common people i stated how there were apps available for people to get involved but it didn t fix the elitist and greed issues in wall street nor did it educate people on prudent investing i stated my intention to develop my skills in leadership through the leadership pathway at wharton gain a formal education in business and economics and spend time at internships so i could get my foot in the door and slowly revolutionize investment banking through ai solutions and new business models to extend access of services to the common people and not high net worth individuals and companies what i didn t say in my app i talked about two things one how i was able to compete in acadec this year we have a lot of people but only from each gpa level get to go so it was tough to get in and how i performed well at the county competition in attendance this year i also talked about how due to a complicated clerical error in how our science olympiad officers organized teams i wasn t put on a team despite doing well on my prelim tests so i wasn t able to compete this year the interviewer seemed interested at some points adding some remarks to the conversation oh so like how a and private stock and that s quite interesting and at other points didn t seem as engaged alright so next question at this point at roughly into the interview the interviewer asked me if i had any questions about penn and this was the portion of conversation which dominated it lasted about minutes i asked a litany of questions about penn i asked about the workload and balance between studies and extracurriculars what the student body was like the most interesting class that the interviewer took it was a for credit course about class structure by throwing the students at some off campus site with a leader some middle class and peasants and told them to work it out for several days the availability of transport into philadelphia and with other cities in the boswash corridor this diverged into a dialogue about train travel and how southern california has the short end of the stick when it comes to traveling how the foreign language and study abroad programs worked i explained how i m passionate for languages and foreign cultures and would like to incorporate that into my study and a few other things this was a much more back and forth conversation in which i really got excited about the school and talked about my enthusiasm for it and my interest in the interviewer s responses some of my comments may have been quite plasticky but i was genuinely interested and wanted to show it it s hard to do that through a phone call without visual cues by the end my interviewer gave some closing remarks he said that he found me to be a smart hardworking individual and that he hopes penn can get back to me quick to take me overall the interview was roughly minutes i thought it went well given the circumstances that it was a phone interview and the field of interest between me and the interviewer didn t line up too much he was very nice and the conversation seemed to go well given this is my first college interview i m unsure as to how this lines up as a college interview so i come to you to give me some feedback given what you may know about penn how penn treats and evaluates interviews and how a college interview is supposed to go how do you think it went for me', 'again i know this post is late but i was writing this information up anyways and figured i d share it here in case it could help anyone this post is a little more in depth than my first one which talks about your mindset and just general tips on how to do well on your interview for anyone still preparing for an upcoming interview and wanting more insight here s a more in depth explanation of the whole process scheduling your interview in most cases highly competitive schools will have an alumni interviewer contact you a few weeks after you ve applied but some schools require you to schedule your own interview weeks before the application deadline for example harvey mudd states on their website that they highly encourage students to schedule an interview before their application deadline this can happen during a campus visit or scheduling online here the point is that you need to check the school s admissions website to see what their policy is if they tell you that an alumnus is going to contact you then you wait for an email after your application is submitted if they want you to schedule the interview yourself and they highly encourage an interview then you need to find the correct channel through which to schedule it when you are scheduling the interview whether online through a portal or via email with alumni consider the timing of the interview itself you want to be accommodating to the interviewer s schedule as they usually also have full time jobs but if it seems like they re open you should avoid early mornings traffic issues end of the day interviewer could be tired or lunchtime interviewer could be hungry to effectively be flexible and judicious in your time slot i would proactively send the interviewer at least three times in the week that fit both your schedules and meet the above criteria but make sure to always prioritize the interviewer s schedule as they are usually busier than you are the interviewer will usually choose a public location to meet like a coffee shop or library so make sure to research where it is and how to get there preparing for the interview like i mentioned in part it s important to properly prepare for your interview through research and practice in particular you want to prepare answers for questions that are most likely to be asked in your interview when i say preparation i don t mean memorization of a specific script this can backfire causing you to come off as robotic in your answers or get flustered if you don t remember the exact phrasing you had in your mock interview just have the main talking points ready to go for the following questions tell me about yourself this is where just about every interview will start and represents a softball for you to get warmed up the answer here isn t too important but it can be a great time to show some personality quirkiness and unique hobbies i usually tell my students to talk about their background a little where they ve grown up the school they go to family etc some hobbies they have the more specific and strange the better and academic interests again specific is better here don t give everything away about yourself here because you ll have plenty of time to talk about your achievements and best qualities but also don t be afraid to show a little about who you are why do you want to attend our school this question probably requires the most research but luckily you may have already done this research in your why us supplemental essay for the school if so just review the reasons in your essay as to why you wanted to attend the school if you wrote the essay properly the reasons should be well researched specific and connect to your admissions angle if the school didn t require a why us essay then you have some work to do you ll have to go to the school s website and find the specific department that you re applying to there you want to try to find any specific courses professors to research with student organizations scholarships or overall educational philosophies that you can connect with the best details to use are ones that allow you to talk about some of your extracurricular or academic achievements for example have you already done some convolutional neural network research at a summer program or with a research mentor try to find an undergraduate research program and professor that specializes in cnn research and talk about how you wish to work with them and bring your own experience to the table if you re unsure whether the detail you researched is sufficiently specific ask yourself if it is something that makes that particular school unique or if it s something that other schools may have as well good food good weather an urban setting etc are not good reasons as these things apply to many schools what will you contribute to the community this question is the ideal place to talk about your leadership roles and experiences during high school you want your answer here to be supported by contributions that you ve already made to your current community because it demonstrates that you ve already had success in following through with your contributions i like to suggest that my students choose one general personality trait or perspective and then at least one specific student organization that they want to get involved in for example a student might say that they want to spread positivity to everyone they interact with at brown in their discourse discussions collaborations and relaxation then they might say that they especially want to bring their positive energy to the engineers without borders chapter there where they can leverage their engineering education to help build crucial infrastructure and machinery for communities in developing countries what makes you unique this question is tough for students because it s difficult to pinpoint a single attribute that makes you unique there are plenty of people with your ethnic background even if you re mixed race plenty of students interested in entrepreneurship or creative writing but in my opinion it s not a single attribute that makes you unique but usually a certain combination of attributes there are probably very few applicants that are mixed race and started their own business and write creative fiction explore the intersection of your interests and you ll usually find something compelling to talk about in addition to preparing your answers to interview questions you also want to prepare every other aspect of the interview to make you feel prepared and confident come up with questions that you want to ask your interviewer for suggestions refer to part arrange your transportation to the interview meetup spot ahead of time if you re driving yourself make sure you ve driven there at least once before the day of the interview update your resume unless your interviewer specifically asks you not to bring one bring a couple of copies of your resume for you both to look at during the interview at the very least you will appear well prepared bring a bottle of water or breath mints these things can help you feel comfortable and confident the day of the interview on the day of you want to make sure to dress the part when choosing an outfit go for something business casual or just a little bit nicer than your everyday clothes i d refrain from showing up in a full suit and tie or really formal dress because the interviewer will probably just be wearing whatever they wear at work but you do want to look good and feel confident so wear something that you think makes you look professional for the timing of the interview itself make sure to double check your email correspondence to confirm the time location and date don t rely on memory if you have an interview set for pm at a local starbucks make sure to leave early enough to account for any unexpected traffic or emergencies the farther the location the more time you should leave as a cushion if you get to the location early i would refrain from messaging the interviewer that you ve arrived minutes early it will put unnecessary pressure on the interviewer to get there early as well spend the time in your car reviewing your supplemental essays and your research on the school for nerves think about doing some power poses no seriously your physical posture has a dramatic effect on your mental state in harvard business school professor amy cuddy famously gave a ted talk entitled your body language may shape who you are in her talk cuddy discusses the power pose which can be used to physically boost your confidence before a nerve wracking task like a job interview you put your hands on your hips and essentially make a superman or wonder woman pose simply by assuming a stance of confidence you can trick your mind into following your body it sounds strange but in her research cuddy discovered that by spending a few minutes in the power pose study subjects were able to boost their own feelings of confidence and perform better in job interviews a private space like the bathroom is the perfect place to do this right before the interview once the interviewer arrives greet them with a handshake look them in the eyes and introduce yourself if you prepared enough the rest should be a piece of cake remember to ask at least a few questions at the end of the conversation this is how you can show demonstrated interest follow up correspondence the day after an interview you should send a quick thank you message to your interviewer to let them know that you appreciate their time and that you genuinely want to attend their alma mater here is a template greet the interviewer by name thank them for their time and express some enthusiasm mention something specific about the interview such as a specific piece of advice the interviewer gave or a particularly interesting topic you discussed encourage them to reach out if they have any questions thank them for their time again example hello jaime i wanted to reach out and say thank you for taking the time to talk with me about university name i particularly enjoyed hearing about your freshman experiences on south campus and our discussions on the role of ai in education if you have any further questions for me please do not hesitate to reach out thanks again for taking the time out of your busy schedule to meet me best regards alex loveless good luck on your interview remember to be yourself and you ll do great', 'back again for more interview tips this post is a little Name: 25_company_startup_offers_offer
Representative_Docs: ['i was an it contractor from till april last year mostly in london for investment banks i then took a perm role which i m leaving next month and have a number of perm offers on the table bear in mind that these are all in holland where i ve been for almost the past four years i m trying to decide between two of the offers my background is net c development and am currently a lead dev experience is mainly backend database sql desktop dev years ago and some web frontend js more recently and also ai ml and devops both offers are for relatively small companies each consisting of around staff company a senior data amp ai engineer consultancy specialising in cloud and ai solutions microsoft stack established nearly years minute commute driving owner around with corporate background formal office environment indefinite contract unusual in holland company b cto consultancy specialising in general full stack development microsoft stack established around months to minute commute driving or train owner around with entrepreneurial background has two other successful companies in same office informal office environment month initial contract that then becomes indefinite usual setup in holland for perm roles both companies are offering similar salaries with promise of increase in jan however company a is offering quantitative revenue sharing and qualitative bonuses as well as little things like laptop allowance phone allowance lease car allowance plus pension it all adds up company b is offering equity that vests at per year over four years however there is scope to negotiate with company b to match or come close to company a s base package they ve already offered a laptop allowance and lease car or transport allowance i may be able to push them up further as they re extremely keen on getting me on board especially after the ceo had a very nice call as he put it with my current manager cfo coo i ve been trying to decide between them for the past couple of weeks and as you can probably imagine i keep flitting between the two i appreciate the above is fairly basic information so feel free to ask me for more info if it will help with advising me my personal interest is cloud and ai as this is where tech is going in the areas i like to work in and this is already exactly company a s specialism with company b i am in the top tech position and the ceo already has cloud on his radar for the company and i will have the opportunity to steer the tech of the company in directions such as ai market and staff resource permitting of course package wise if company b is successful then the stock after four years could be extremely lucrative but of course that s a risk the ceo is very bullish and his company is effectively a supplier to one of his miles', 'hello all i am working on a beer recipe generator that uses observed real world recipes to create a model and allows a user to generate their own recipe based on flavor descriptors and the real world model i have posted about this in the past algorithm help similar to knapsack problem but i don t know how to model my current problem against existing solutions hop flavor calculator design assistance and opinions right now i am using google s cp sat constraint problem solver to take my real world model and the user s desired input and spit out possible recipes the constraint model would look something like this max unique ingredients ingredient type ingredient type ingredient type toasty taste out of i feed this in i get out a list of possible recipes on my frontend the user can continue to narrow down the model by adding more constraints toasty of caramel of etc i have a model for each beer style based on thousands of real world recipes i know very little about data science so i feel like i m missing something i posted about this project here a few days ago and had a few data scientists and ml guys tell me how they would solve this problem but their responses went way over my head there were some suggestions about using machine learning to build a model but it seems like ml is more for predictive analysis rather than designing a solution based on a learned model there are lots of ml examples for figuring out if a picture is a t shirt or a pair of pants but there aren t many tutorials for using ml to generate a picture of pants although i suppose this is what deepfakes are should i continue using data analysis to generate static models that i feed into a constraint problem solver or is there a better way to solve this also anyone good with stats know if there is something more i should be doing on that end when generating my models right now i analyze every recipe in a style and spit out the following average unique ingredient count for the style ingredient category usage average unique ingredient count average usage percent range std ingredient usage percent range only for recipes that include that ingredient std average taste descriptor ranges std i m not doing any kind of complicated analysis such as if ingredient x usage is above ingredient y usage is typically below or something like that i m not sure if i could even feed data like that into the cp sat tool but i feel like that s the kind of stuff an ml model would uncover', 'i m work in research and i work on an app that s attempting to estimate daily calories with pictures we ve tried going down this route without using machine learning for about two years with little to no success i started looking into solutions and i felt like the obvious answer was to use ml cv granted no one in my lab is a computer science person our freelance app developer has no specialization in ml and our back end guy has zero interest in pursuing it we even have an ai contact researching ai in heart and breast cancer research that flat out said he didn t want to tackle food recognition it seemed like a dead end with cv being unrealistic for our project my lab deals with pictures of food on a daily basis people send us pictures of food and we analyze them we have probably years worth of pictures from studies i mean terabytes of photos in our system they re all labeled by the fndds food and nutrition database for dietary studies food codes and assigned ratings calories macros micros etc by people specially trained to visually estimate food in my opinion if any lab has the resources to tackle food image computer vision problems it d be us we would have to do the bounding boxes as this was never considered before but as stated above no one other than me is interested in this i decided to learn how to program about months ago when i first thought of this i m not trying to reinvent the wheel and create my own algorithms i ve read enough papers to know that food image recognition volume estimation and calorie estimation is a difficult task for ml i just read papers and try to implement the code from github it s so frustrating because i feel like every paper implements a new framework whether it be tensorflow pytorch darknet matlab octave etc if anyone s wondering i tried yolo v wiser and musefood i was going to try nutrinet deepfood but i didn t feel like trying to learn caffe i don t have time to do this at work because my job is just data entry and it s not like my other projects stop because i decided to try to learn ml since i do data entry i listen to a lot of courses across the different platforms like coursera udacity youtube udemy etc while i work i have a basic understanding of cv ml and various frameworks obviously i m no expert but i ve watched enough classes and tutorials that each one kind of fills in the gaps of the others i still have no idea what s going on when i reach the technical aspect of the papers however when i try to implement the github code it feels like everything is deprecated and the code doesn t even work despite the paper being published at most years ago going through debugging code to make it work after doing hours of data entry is a bit exhausting to be quite honest it feels like data entry is debugging excel sheets more than it is entering data additionally with each paper s code being written in a new framework it feels like i m starting from square one and i have to relearn the basics each time usually the readmes are cryptic at best and the language in the papers don t really help me out since it s written for a much more cs educated audience i kept bringing up different papers i ve read that i thought would improve what we were doing based on reported accuracy they pretty much all got dismissed i was told that a smartphone couldn t handle machine learning it can with tensorflow lite coreml but that s not the point so i said okay then we ll use it on our computers then what started as a cool idea to get our app to be better slowly turned into a thought of how to improve our whole data management system i tried to implement the above codes to present the proof of concept my adhd brain kept thinking i should try this one no this one is more accurate no this one is specific to food etc i was having a real hard time getting through some of the code and i was getting really frustrated from it not working that also contributed to me jumping from paper to paper i found an article on yolov on food i felt like it was my hello world that i finally got to work i wanted to present a proof of concept to my boss to say this is worth pursuing i was super excited i got yolov to work with the uecfood data set after reading an article implementing it getting darknet working on my computer took me forever to even get working let alone get everything set up to get the code to run downloading the data set and training it i even went as far as getting different apple pictures putting bounding boxes around them labeling them properly and training it how to recognize an apple which isn t part of the original training set i was so excited that it worked i came up with a plan on how to implement it in our system i presented how i thought we could overcome the shortcomings of ml cv in this area using questionnaires that are already standard in our studies i even made a website using django that was a crappy clone of our current website with user logins authentications separate homepages for participants vs admins photo uploads and more that i was going to try to use the algorithm in i even made a script to scrape pictures of the apples from google i learned databases with mysql and sqlite so that i could be able to search the fndds for the foods needed i showed my coworkers and no one seemed to care i even got made fun of for it in a meeting i bought a pretty nice computer with a really good gpu and decent cpu to pursue this more seriously i feel silly that i even tried to learn this and put my own money time and effort even sillier that i tried to implement it in a professional environment but i am still passionate about learning this i really think that if i could get this to work it would make our lab so much more productive and in turn make my life of debugging excel sheets easier i do this work because i m passionate about it i feel like if we solve this problem then the doors open to even more research being done the papers i ve read seem to be progressing becoming more accurate again i don t totally understand the technical aspects of these papers just basing it on reported accuracy outside of research it also would help solve frustrations with tracking apps myfitnesspal loseit etc there are already apps that exist caloriemama but it only does image classification and not very accurately outside of basic foods i feel like there s no point in pursuing this at work though i just haven t felt more passion for something in a long time it makes me want to go back to do a master phd in ml on this topic i ve read that machine learning graduate programs are so over saturated since everyone wants to do it i did my undergraduate in kinesiology amp dietetics i would have to go back and do so many prerequisites to even be considered anyway i doubt i d even be taken seriously if i applied to a masters program my lab offers free tuition for job related courses at the university my lab is associated with there s very strict rules about it however based off how receptive they have been to me presenting this to them i doubt they d approve me going to take actual classes i just generally feel disheartened by it all it feels like i ve spent so many hours learning this only to be completely shot down time and time again i don t really know what to do moving forward i don t really know why i made this post it started out as a question as to how better navigate the github pages of these papers to not have to deal with all the deprecation and version issues i ve been dealing with but as i typed it turned more into a rant of my frustrations at work i don t really have anyone to talk to about this sort of thing whenever i bring up programming at this point everyone s ears kind of shut if anyone could offer words of encouragement or advice on moving forward i d appreciate it']

Name: 30_linguistics_nlp_cognitive_cognitive science
Representative_Docs: ['linguistics is a pretty rarefied field not a lot of schools even have full linguistics departments if you love it do it though i majored in linguistics though my masters was in tesol which is sometimes packaged under applied linguistics and in the nearly decades since i graduated which includes about years of college teaching i have only ever taught one linguistics course but to be fair i veered away from pure linguistics a long time ago linguistics does however offer a variety of in roads with other disciplines and there s a lot of opportunity in coding ai corpus linguistics speech therapy and even medical research i e brain injury head trauma etc or of course education i apply what i learned to teaching and working with english language learners i love love love sociolinguistics and i found it invaluable in my high school and college teaching careers working in cities with large populations of immigrants if you full on into socio you will also wade in the waters of sociology anthropology ethnic studies and history it really is a field that gives one a big picture understanding of how the human condition works not sure i actually gave you anything of value in all my rambling but it s not often one comes across someone in the wild who says they re down with linguistics', 'as a current ma student in linguistics who has recently decided not to pursue a phd for many of the same reasons you ve listed here though i m still coming to terms to that decision because academia has been my dream for so long if linguistics is something you re passionate about pursuing there certainly are phd programs that would happily accept you even with only a minor if you have a strong and clear vision of how you could succeed in research once you arrive in the program all of the programs i ve looked at even consider students who ve never studied linguistics at all as long as you can advocate for how you have general academic skillsets and if your background is in something relevant to what you want to do research on e g people who studied comp sci in undergrad who now want to go into computational linguistics so if that s the only thing stopping you from applying for phd programs in linguistics don t let that hold you back that being said the job market in linguistics isn t any better than the job market in any other academic field and there aren t a whole lot of industry jobs in linguistics unless you go into nlp stuff the only non nlp industry job i ve ever heard of for linguistics phd holders is curriculum development at duolingo which might be cool but isn t exactly a reliable back up plan for if academia doesn t work out', 'gt yeah i mentioned previously that it s debatable whether linguistics falls under cog sci but people like chomsky do think so regardless nlp specifically is intertwined enough to linguistics that wiki categorizes it as a sub field of linguistics i didn t say linguistics wasn t under cognitive science i said if the only connection between cognitive science and nlp is linguistics falls under cognitive science then that s very tenuous connection cognitive science is so a large of a field that even if you say this most of it will still be irrelevant and even if we restrict our view to the intersection between cognitive science and nlp that still makes up a very small part of nlp research cognitive science is literally completely irrelevant to of nlp research so telling this guy that his degree in cog sci and cs might be useful because cog sci is pretty relevant to nlp is really bad advice cognitive science is not pretty relevant to nlp they are almost entirely disjoint subjects that have a small intersection']

Name: 32_passion_career_development_web
Representative_Docs: ['you don t need a computer science specific one what you should do is get a therapist to help you work on your personal issues with high expectations and how you feel not achieving them and talk to a mentor in cs maybe someone from your school some senior you know or else look online for mentor matching in your area about possibilities for your future gt i invested years in android dev and yet today i couldn t think of next billion dollar idea very few people have the next billion dollar idea and fewer see it to fruition this should probably not be your goal it s definitely not necessary to be either successful or happy or even to be above average gt i was making small apps but was never able to create a real big one this is completely normal big apps are created by teams of hundreds of people people often credit one person for inventing something but you have to remember that it was then built upon by others it may have been revolutionary but the original version probably sucks to use gt i am bad at modern android development basically am bad at being a great idea thinker learner everything but i still think i will do better in android environment in amazon than a guy who just landed there with knowledge of his coding skills the thing with companies is that they value not only domain knowledge android environment in this case but languages and inevitably there will be tools you need to learn in any new job especially in faang knowledge is valuable to them but tbh you will learn a lot more in however many years you stay with Name: 33_ruby_python_language_languages
Representative_Docs: ['i was looking at the popularity trends of some programming languages and something that caught my eye is the clear decline in ruby s popularity it made me wonder what did ruby do wrong i personally don t do ruby but in the past i did and i liked it very much i would say it has the nicest syntax i ve ever seen ml s based languages have a beautiful syntax too of course python is also very simple but i don t get why python over ruby was the one that made it to the moon i remember when python and ruby were pretty much in the same league what did python offer that ruby didn t why most machine learning and data science libraries were built for python if ruby has similar capabilities and can have bindings to c personally i believe coding scripts are more fun to write in ruby than in python now i thought this was due to ruby being regarded as slow but i come to across graalvm and they claim that truffleruby ruby on graalvm is by far the fastest runtime to execute ruby code in this talk benchmarks show truffleruby is x times faster than cruby so ruby s slowness shouldn t be the reason for its decline i don t see it s decline trend being corrected yes ruby is not dead by any means it is still in the top most popular languages but at this rate it will become the new php and php the new perl and perl well is almost dead looking at the trend how can we say ruby is a safe bet what did ruby do wrong', 'as someone has pointed out it s not true that no beginners are learning ruby as usual a statement that absolute is bound to be wrong you just need one person learning ruby as a beginner and that statement is wrong many bootcamps are in ruby probably because the people lost jobs doing ruby on rails anyway to me although somewhat different philosophically python and ruby do roughly the same thing i went to a ruby conference many years ago in the late s i think and suggested they make a push to teach ruby in us high schools that would have probably done a lot to promote the language anyway guido van rossum inventor of python was hired by google and python s popularity grew there it was also the language used by data scientist data visualization folks even some machine learning it has two web frameworks at least associated with it django and flask there are statistical packages similar to r which is another programming language these could have been developed for ruby but apparently ruby didn t seek this out so right now python is much more popular than ruby so if you ask why people don t learn ruby it s probably because you have two thoughts when it comes to learning a language how hard is it to learn and can i get a job with that language the second is probably quite influential if you just want to learn to program there s no reason you couldn t start with ruby it s just that you d probably have to pick up a second language i suspect because of the popularity of python people just aren t willing to expend the time to develop new ruby resources', 'welcome new programmer i think you re thinking about things from the wrong perspective suppose you want to buy a car maybe you re looking for an affordable door sedan toyota has the corolla honda has a civic nissan has a sentra ford has well i don t know the ford line maybe the focus no one ever asks why are there so many cars each of them must do something different so many beginners think that the reason there are so many programming languages is because each one is good at something they don t think of it like cars that many languages pretty much do the same thing then they ask why do you need so many languages if they all do the same thing and i reply then why is there a toyota honda nissan ford chevy why do you need so many car companies why not just one and you d think well i want choice even if they all produce cars and trucks and suvs i don t want to just have one choice for example to me ruby and python are the same language i mean they aren t but they tend to be used for roughly the same purpose however python is at least three times as popular is that because it s a better language if peter pan peanut butter is the best selling peanut butter does that make it best maybe some small company from whereever makes the best peanut butter whatever that means several reasons python is popular first many libraries were written for that language in areas such as data visualization numerical algorithms machine learning and so forth once a language is popular others also use it and there s a lot of material out there why wasn t ruby so popular it could have been that language there s nothing inherently special about python maybe for a twist of fate ruby would be the language for numerical analysis data visualization machine learning etc two other reasons perhaps ruby never made much of a push to be adopted at universities or high schools as an alternative to java not sure python did either but some cs departments who didn t care for java adopted python again it could have been ruby but they picked python when you have a large number of students learning it then it gets more popular in the s video recorders vcrs had two data formats vhs and betamax betamax was a sony product and by all accounts it produced superior quality videos but vhs could store videos at lesser quality for more hours and the people who owned vcrs opted for vhs let s address relevancy too many programmers say i hear this language is going obsolete so why should i learn it without doing much research they take the word of one person and now they re scared they are wasting their time no one would say cobol is popular everyone has been saying it s practically a dead language and yet if you learn it you could probably find a job you might have to move to some location to get that job but there are still cobol programmers out there when a codebase contains lines of code people rarely decide yeah that s ditch all that code and rewrite it from scratch every years forever that s why a popular language like java with gazillions of lines of code is likely to survive and anyone saying it s going obsolete doesn t know what they re talking about you would think a company like microsoft would have rewritten microsoft word a dozen times from scratch by now but there s probably lines of code that are years old in their codebase and in years there are likely to be lines of code years old that still run people don t rewrite code easily it s a lot of labor to do so finally to address your question python has a lot of libraries python seems easier to learn avoids forcing you to learn oo concepts like java and c but honestly it s relevant because a lot of people use it seriously why is english relevant what is this language particularly good at compared to other languages i m not saying languages don t have some strengths people use c c because they run fast compared to python but most languages run fast enough fast enough that you can t tell for the most part functional languages avoid issues with side effects erlang scales to handle large numbers of processes javascript got associated with web development but it s a bit unfair to think of these languages each having their own purpose just like it s unfair to think the honda civic and toyota corolla were built for different purposes because why would two companies essentially build the same car because they can']
