Name: 37_cs_ai_major_minor
Representative_Docs: ['it s a bad idea to specialize too much at the undergraduate level ai is graduate level discipline any ai position where you re actually performing ai research will require a masters if not a phd true ai employees are computer scientists emphasis on the scientist part there are a lot of psuedo ai positions that are really just statistics with some programming thrown in a bs in ai will get you those but i have a feeling you don t want that in addition most positions of this type are interdisciplinary as in the employer expects you to have the broader knowledge from a broad undergraduate degree like computer science all of these specialized degrees like ai try to shortcut that but all it does is just makes you a weaker candidate compared to the person with a bs in cs and a ms phd in data science ai a broader degree also gives you the flexibility to branch into other fields in case your initial plan doesn t work out having options is everything in life with a bs in cs you ll still learn everything you need to be a good ai scientist as well as having the option to branch out to other applications of cs you can theoretically find a well paying job with a b in ai but it ll be more difficult and like stated before it ll unlikely be actual ai it ll be theoretically possible to do a masters in cs after a bs in ai but it would require the ai degree to have all of the pre requisites you re just better off doing an actual cs undergraduate here s a typical education to career progression for a ai engineer scientist bscompe ms or phd in cs math bscs ms or phd in cs math bsstatistics ms or phd in cs math bsmath ms or phd in cs math the most important thing for ai is a undergraduate and postgraduate combination of math or computer science preferably both', 'i briefly looked through your history and noticed you have more years in undergrad if you re scared that you don t have a solid foundation then start building one you can consider machine learning as spicy statistics applied mathematics first make sure you address the pros and cons of pursuing a graduate degree and if you re actually interested in the subject matter you may or may not be that person and i could be flat out wrong but make sure you don t idealize a higher degree or a specific subject matter until you actually try it and take into account your finances and time costs some options apply for a minor in cs math stats take courses that you think will be foundational apply for a dual in above this one is likely less feasible if you want to graduate on time and could also depend on your work ethic and how your school sets curricula take foundational courses as electives in the topics you re interested in that are once again foundational self study pick one of all will lead you to your goal unless you re absolutely sure you want to get into machine learning and artificial intelligence i d pick make sure you plan out your credits graduating on time or early and fit in interesting classes around that you should also talk to your advisor about which courses to take to help you transition to help you determine what courses may be foundational depending on your school you should be able to add a minor after you take courses so is pretty much the same as the minor is really more for name if you re ee i m assuming you ve likely taken a multivariable calculus course and you ve had an introduction to probability if you re absolutely sure of pursuing a graduate degree the courses out of the scope of your major that i d recommend looking into are an introductory proof based course linear algebra which is likely going to be a lot of theory and proofs any sort of introduction to ml ai courses which tend to offer a more shallow overview of the material and an introductory optimization course if you can fit in more advanced course s senior year that d be ideal i think by the time you get there though you ll have a better feel of what you want to do i m not very familiar with m s programs directly with machine learning but m s cs degrees tend to be flexible along with applied math and stats depending on the schools you apply to generally i d say an m s in cs is a safe bet as for me i m doing an m s under applied mathematics and i d say about of my courses revolve around ml and the other are analysis optimization courses which in a sense also are somewhat ml oriented i think in your scenario you re getting a bit ahead of yourself imo you should first address if you re seriously interested in the subject matter i m sure there s a little overlap but are you sure that you re interested in ml even though you ve been taking courses in ee even before that if you haven t taken the foundational courses for a field how can you be sure of an m s or even a phd in the field and then address if you need a higher degree for your career goals', 'to be more specific than other commenters here s what you want to do ai is mostly about machine learning and math unified with computer science so in hs you should go out of your way to expose yourself to computer science and math topics aim for the highest ap level math classes even if you struggle with them cause if you fail you can just retake in college and it ll be easier like i took calculus bc ap in my school i m now lol but it ll be different for you maybe Name: 42_physics_phd_physics major_major
Representative_Docs: ['i m currently a first year phd student in computational condensed matter physics i m hoping to get out of this posting some advice on whether i m in the right field with regards to my passions and desired career goals when i started undergraduate i was genuinely interested in physics and i loved immersing myself in all the theory math and problem solving techniques i m also very happy with my bs so no regrets there over the years however i also started developing acute interest in ai and all its sub fields machine learning statistical learning etc although i don t have an official cs background i was still able to read academic papers books and more or less understand the theory and the implementations this way i learned more about the field and suddenly i am in love with it my desire career goal i love that at heart ai is a theoretical subject in which my knowledge of physics think spin glass systems and mathematics are readily relevant i also love that it is an inherently coding based field i love to code and most importantly i love how it can be directly applied in the industry to build cool stuff think self driving cars and help others think speech recognition based on all these at the moment it is my desire and decision to land a research oriented ai job in the industry for example a research position at google deepmind the problem i don t see the everyday value in physics projects i m doing my phd in physics in all honesty i m somewhat bored by my current research project because i just don t see the value of it and even when i talk to my colleagues i fail to see how any of our research projects can have a big picture impact in the everyday world not just the scientific community exclusively i want to immerse myself in the field of ai but no time on top of that i have found myself putting a lot of academic papers and books in ai on my to do list i have also cooked up some ml projects for myself to work on but being a physics phd and a ta there s barely any free time i have for myself to indulge in the above i m at a disadvantage with my phd with respect to my career goal although i believe that a physicist can in principle get a job as a researcher in ai what are the odds physics is only tangentially related to ai with the common denominator of math and problem solving with this phd i d still be at a disadvantage compared to someone who did a phd in cs with ai focus questions for those of you in the ai field with a physics phd what was the process like did you feel like you were at a disadvantage did you have to go way outside the physics discipline and do extra stuff in order to qualify as a ai researcher candidate when i process the above in my head i cannot help but think that a cs phd would perhaps be better suited for me it seems to be in accord with my desires and it would have a direct line of contact with my career goals but i ve invested a lot of time in the physics track and abandoning it so suddenly makes me feel like i m making an irrational decision based on a few at the moment circumstances i m not happy with that s why i wanted to get your opinions on this so what do you guys think', Name: 45_machine learning_machine_learning_ml
Representative_Docs: ['the trend in ai is to have hot spots that end up being pretty useless i recall around a decade ago it was social network analysis enough time has passed that we can pretty safely say very little useful insight came out of that field after alpha zero deep reinforcement learning was hot no one has yet found any use for them outside of playing video games the faithful may want to give it a few more years before giving up the ghost but i d put down money that no one will some of the new hot things that will probably also be dead ends are in my estimation gans fairness in machine learning and explainable ai on the other hand a new thing that probably will have staying power is transformers for sequential data', 'comparing machine learning to data analysis is a bit like comparing a textbook or literature to a dictionary each has an author and carries meaning but their utility and context are very different you can know every word in every language but you re not much until you can combine them in a way that conveys meaning as to whether machine learning is hyped sure it is evolving quickly enough that its different facets and applications are emerging in legitimate uses at a fairly brisk pace we re seeing forty years of anticipation and hard work start to show amazing results not to mention many emergent traits that couldn t have been anticipated it seems to be in the early stages of a growth explosion comparable to the internet in the late s and the smart phone talk about a misnomer in the late naughts hype just means that people are excited about what they re finding will everyone use it to radically transform their lives into epic journeys of global mastery nope will it make the next years as different from today as now is different from the s probably most of the impressiveness that we ll see looking back from say at machine learning now is probably lost on us at the moment that s just part of limitations of humans being in the midst of radical change that doesn t mean though that you shouldn t embrace it if you think you might have something to offer in the process of it emerging', 'right but that means all these data scientists and machine learning engineers aren t actually engineering more efficient machine learning algorithms they re just stacking wrapper layers that someone else built the same goes for any transformations or other compute heavy processes they employ so really the bulk of practitioners aren t contributing to the speed up power efficiency of machine learning just a few more talented engineers i d wager of self proclaimed data scientists are just blowing raw data sets through automl of some form and waiting for whatever comes out the other end with no idea how or why it got there the tools are convenient but they are not power efficient it s just a passive exercise waiting for someone else to release a pretrained model to freeze the last few layers or use some prepackaged model from microsoft that isn t built for their specific use case it s the difference between bolting on a cold air intake to a honda civic and calling it a race car and being a proper engineer designing and milling custom components for a formula car sure the honda is accessible and easy and makes someone feel like they re going fast but it is not a race car the same goes for strapping on python models of course non massive tech company data scientists aren t going to do the work of refactoring these things into pure c c implementations they ll just lean on the idea that python is just running precompiled c c and that s fast i mean how many of this deluge of machine learning engineers are actually porting the python implementations into pure c after prototyping i d bet none because for of applications it s currently good enough to pay for the python interpreter power bill and python dev kids are cheaper than c engineers working in the roots of these things even cheaper are bi analysts with no development experience or training throwing extremely filthy data through prebuilt azure pipelines i dunno maybe you people don t pay your own electric bills or are wealthy enough that you don t notice on the months you re trying to train models at home nor have you had to weight the costs of on prem gpu farms because the regulated nature of your industry won t easily allow piping npi or pci data through cloud machine learning solutions without providing the results won t expose elements of training data nor the service provider use your data in reinforcement training models provided for use by other clients just to fluff the marketing executives micro boner he s swinging around the office because he learned the phrase machine learning over the weekend despite the company not being able to afford an enterprise sql server to even store enough data to do work with in the first place']

Name: 46_python_data science_python data_data
Representative_Docs: ['jose portilla has some excellent content on udemy for both learning python as well as data science and machine learning they re in between sales right now so course prices are high i d wait until they run another sale you can pick these up for like bucks a course data science from scratch by joel grus and python for data analysis by wes mckinney are amazing books they cover an intro to python and then go into things like pandas numpy etc that are basically essential libraries when working with data in python both books recently released a nd edition which i would recommend picking up because they use python instead of', 'to learn python i suggest the mitx x and x on edx these two classes will give a very solid foundation in python and actually at the end of x the class goes over a very basic overview of data science and machine learning and you will do a data science project but it does not go into depth once you have a solid foundation in python i suggest doing jose portilla data science and machine learning course boot camp on udemy it will give you a good overview of the field and you will do several capstone project i am doing this course right and i am half way through it is pretty good course and i think after the course you will know where you want to go in the field and how to develop your skills and the kind of projects you need to do if you want to use python for data science i suggest developing good foundation in the language for data science purposes python is just a tool some people use r but each language has its advantage and disadvantage but it seems that the majority prefer python for data science and machine learning', 'probably start with learning python as it s a tad more common than r and stackoverflow questions answers are much much better though both are good and both are free stackoverflow and those sorts of site sites are a regular go to a lot of people will have a strong opinion on one or the other but in reality both are pretty good get to know how to use either jupiter notebooks r markdown or google colab all very similar but useful tools for sharing your work in r and python i personally prefer python tableau is a good program and a decent amount of places use it but you gotta pay if i recall and it s more for visualisation than data science per se not sure about the others as for courses on udemy coursera i d highly recommend zero to mastery machine learning i m not affiliated with them in any way but have done the course and it s very good starts from the very beginning and has practical examples you work through with code additionally look at some of the top rated kaggle kernels kaggle is a site that has worked examples for various data science competitions most of which have companies offering big money as prizes many of them are very comprehensive and well explained and they have code in both r and python and more you can download data for free and get some practise with different kinds of problems also has very specific free mini courses for areas of data science you might want to skill up in it s an amazing resource use it basic skills would be changing data into various formats different types and shapes pivot tables and grouping and sub setting data that sort of manipulation transformation is used a lot for both analysis and visualisation if you don t already know brush up on some statistics too correlations anova t test granger causality etc useful when doing data science and analysis analysis wise basic skills would be classification regression clustering problems and then using various techniques to improve them grid search genetic algorithms encoding categorical variables dimension reduction potentially also neural networks and computer vision stuff depending on exactly what you re interested in visualisation wise don t just learn how to plot graphs line graphs bar charts histograms heap maps etc learn how to make them pretty i e fonts spacing colors and interactive e g plotly and similar packages learn how to make it into a function so you can make an awesome graph in essentially a single line of code look at various publications and try to reproduce their figures some other general useful sites for analytics and visualisation are machine learning mastery towards data science medium and pyimageseaech the last one is specifically for computer vision as for careers you just need a foot in the door the fastest way to do that in my opinion is to try and get a job at a non government organisation as a data scientist or researcher or analyst terms are generally interchangeable will give similar results on non sites ngo tend to accept people with wide ranges of skill sets know a bunch of people who transitioned into data science roles without really having a background in the area be willing to not make big such money immediately it will come with time but for the most part when you re changing careers you really just as you said need a foot in the door also once you have a foundation of skills start doing some basic projects that interest you and put them on github not strictly necessary but still good learning github is another good hard skill to have hope this helps best of luck']

Name: 51_phd_research_phds_salary
Representative_Docs: ['gt wondering if a phd in ai will delay me from starting a career before and enjoying the other fruits of personal life such as travelling getting married etc i m a phd student in cs but not ai specifically but this is general enough for me to answer i think delayed career yes although if you enter straight out of undergrad it s closer to years is average if you want to do a phd i d recommend just going straight into a program if you can you can usually leave with a masters if things go badly you don t like it sometimes starting a phd with a pre existing masters can result in a longer program due to needing extra courses that didn t transfer taking generals quals travelling not really people still travel a lot conferences are all over the place and i see plenty of people going on trips around the world in my own lab it s common to extend conference travel into mini vacations flights to from the conference are usually paid for along with hotel for the specified dates anything extra you pay for now you won t be staying in star hotels or anything crazy like that but if you like inexpensive vacations like hiking nature stuff you can still see go to lots of places on your own as well getting married i know plenty of phd students who are in their s and married as well as people slightly above your age in serious relationships of all genders university dating scenes can sometimes suck depending on the school but many schools are near major metropolitan areas i will say it s easy to fuck up relationships as a phd student because you spend so much time working this can be mitigated by selecting a good phd advisor lots of other posts on this topic can be found online and setting healthy work hours for yourself money you will get less money than people who just did an undergraduate degree on average but your job will be more interesting if you like to work on new and interesting problems the opportunity cost gt however i can t seem to find a love of just purely researching ai phenomena or dealing with pure stats all day i find the application interesting and if i find i can apply the tech to solving a real life problem that can be applied within my lifetime then that is what drives me if this is true you should not do a phd in ai ml essentially all of the interesting research in ai ml requires some minimal understanding of theory without working at that level you re just glueing shit together in pytorch tensorflow which isn t really cs ml ai research there are non cs research fields where applied ml is much more research worth think biology applied sciences etc but that would no longer be a cs phd gt however a phd seems like a full commitment to research and partially academia yeah that s exactly what it is although i know plenty of people who do pure research and drop the academia part all together i know extremely few people from my program who go to quant finance not because they can t because they all get research scientist positions at google fb microsoft and that s what they really want for the most part all in all if all you care about is money and fancy vacations just working for a big n quant company right out of undergrad is the optimal choice assuming you achieve an average outcome you ll outdo your peers in finance by quite a bit and have a better life than them if you do this don t bother with an ms they re not really worth a lot for regular software engineers reading this post gives me the impression you care a lot about money and status doesn t automatically make you bad person btw most people are like this and not a lot about solving new and difficult problems', 'hello everyone hope this is an ok place to ask i m a year old woman in copenhagen denmark and i m you could go for cyber security you will be using cyber security as monitoring traffic and feeding into machine learning and its a vast growing research technique', 'i was actually in both roles amp x b swe in cybersecurity can create infra think ansible k s docker golang python networking or product for a cybersecurity company i e in scala java golang something enterprise y they can potentially do more depending on what team you re on in a cyber security company but usually if you re a backend front end software engineer you d be working on whatever domain knowledge you have plus the added benefit of applying some cyber security knowledge to your domain amp x b i have had coworkers who did not have to know any cyber security at all to be a software engineer in cybersecurity it always depends on the team more often if your team is within a research org or aligns closely to the data science machine learning side then you ll apply more cyber security to your job line amp x b now a product security engineer can be two things in my opinion there definitely can be more either you work on the core product security for products being developed at your company or you work on ensuring that your company s product is safely guarded by past present and future attacks the latter requires much more research and threat knowledge the former requires working knowledge of things like oss dast sast threat modeling etc and of course they can work on both oftentimes you wouldn t be writing new software but you would be writing automation scripts internal tooling things that can scale the product security reviews for your team in case your company wants to scale out it s product range amp x b either can help your career i probably have multiple opinions depending on what you want to do more contributor more architectural etc but since you said you want to be in a management role relating to cyber security you ll probably want to work in product security engineering the only downside here is you wouldn t be working on generalist swe things that a typical swe would work on at a cybersecurity company i would want to see what team function the cyber security company wants their swes to do though it may offer you the best of both worlds']

Name: 54_pixels_opencv_image_computer vision
Representative_Docs: ['what s a dot your computer doesn t know what that is you mean you re looking for is a small roughly circular region that is dark you can figure that out by converting the rgb pixels in your image to hsl and checking the lightness there s formulas for that has high contrast between it and the surrounding pixels on the border and low contrast between it and the surrounding pixels within the regions that s closer to what a computer could understand but still not quite precise enough to turn into a program you d have to define a bunch of thresholds first for things like small circular dark high contrast and low contrast because your computer doesn t know any of that out of the box computer vision is a hard problem there s a reason why self driving cars made by some of the brightest minds in the industry are still having trouble reading road signs this sort of project would be a great intro to computer vision problems but if you re just learning to program i recommend focusing on that first and then getting to image stuff only after you ve gotten all the basics down', 'i m a senior in computer science and my most proficient languages are python and javascript i m taking a computer vision course that s taught in matlab the professor has mentioned a final project due in december but has yet to reveal many details so i m not sure if making a matlab i took a python course a few years ago that touched on computer vision so i m familiar with the general basics but i have never coded in matlab before are there any nintendo ds emulators that would be better than others for working with for a project like this i currently use desmume is matlab capable of controlling an emulator or would i need something to translate between the two lastly how insane does this project sound what type of hour commitment am i looking at because i don t know much about computer vision or functionality of matlab i m not sure if it has the amount of built in functions that would make my idea of a final project a cake walk or nearly impossible amp x b the general flow of what i was thinking to accomplish this project would be say we re playing pokemon diamond and we re on route the four possible pokemon you can encounter are starly bidoof shinx and kricketot i would load pictures of these four pokemon and their shiny versions into the program i would place my pokemon trainer character into a patch of grass and then want the program to slowly walk in a circle so it always stays in the grass then when we encounter a pokemon the program identifies which pokemon we encountered either by reading its name or comparing the image to the pre loaded images as part of the identifying it determines if it is shiny if not the player runs from the battle and begins walking in a circle again if it is shiny the program would either end but set off an alarm to let me know that it found one or fight and capture it that decision would rely on how easy it ends up being to control the emulator amp x b i m in the brainstorming phase so literally any and all suggestions comments and questions are welcome', 'one building block you are missing that often isn t explained well in beginner tutorials is libraries python and most other languages provides a number of tools for doing things that you can t directly do with variables loops lists and classes for example print but also tools for opening and working with files connecting to things on the internet etc etc you can find out about all that by searching python standard library then you and other programmers take those tools and create your own tools that build off of them into more complex tools libraries you can use programs like pip to install and use them in your own code but in a technical sense it s not strictly necessary what they are doing you could do on your own given enough time the image recognition program one is kind of hard because machine learning is a very advanced field but i ll try and talk you through how i might approach it knowing only about the tools in the python standard library no machine learning first i d need to figure out how to work with a picture i ll assume that i want to work with pictures stored in files jpeg files png files etc i know that python can open files and give you access to the information inside i could google for a python library that will open image files for you and learn to use that alternatively i could look at the features built in to python and see that there is a function called open that opens files it appears to have a mode that opens text files and binary files images aren t text files so they must be binary files i ll start with just png files the wikipedia page for it explains what is in a png file and how that binary data is a compressed version of an image and how that compression works all that s necessary for this to work is the ability to compare numbers to each other loop through a list of numbers and move bits of data around which i can easily do with my basic python tools so with that knowledge i know i can write a program that opens a png file and converts it to an image as a list of lists of pixels and their colors now i need to figure out how to take a list of lists of pixels and detect things in it for starters i might try to detect a ball in a picture i know that it will be easier to detect the ball if it is a different color from its surroundings but i also know that there will be shadows and shading and stuff so the ball probably won t be exactly the same color so maybe i write some code that looks at every pixel and instead of the millions of possible colors rounds that color to one of i don t know different values that s fairly straightforward with standard python tools but i could expand on it a bit if needed now i m not looking at millions of pixels that each could be millions of different values i m looking at millions of pixels that each could be one of values i imagine that a ball will be a group of pixels that are almost all the same color and that that group of pixels will be round in shape same color is easy to check for since we ve simplified the colors a lot i ll say that an object is any group of pixels in the image that are all adjacent to each other and all the same color so i write code that goes through the pixels of the image and for each one of them gathers up any adjacent pixels that are the same color into a group maybe a list then i ll have a bunch of lists of objects in the image things that are the same color and contiguous next i need to check if one of these objects is a ball which i ve decided means that it is round i do some googling on that and realize that round things have the property that all of their parts are at most some distance from the center of the round thing and that nothing else is closer to the center than the parts of the round thing are that gives me a few things i need to do for one thing i need to find the center of each group i ll just average the x and y coordinates of each group that s pretty easy then i need to figure out the maximum distance from the center of any one pixel in the group i can find a simple equation for the distance between two points and apply it to each pixel in the group and the center then take the maximum of those finally i need to check if every pixel that is that distance from the center is a part of the group to give myself a better chance at succeeding i ll just say that of the pixels within that distance from the center need to be part of the group so i go through each group and each radius and center for each group and look at all of the pixels in the image that are within that distance from the center if gt of them are part of the group i ve found a ball if none of the groups have all of these properties then i have not found a ball now i ve got some code that takes a png image file and maybe can pick out a ball in it it s not a general image recognition program but the important thing is that each part of this program could be replaced with different code that expands its functionality after the first step here it didn t matter where the image came from all i cared about was that i had a dimensional list of pixels so i could replace the first step with code that gets that information from jpegs and the rest of the code would still work and now i ve built it into a program that can handle both png and jpeg or i could add code that looks for lines and now i have a program that can find balls and lines i could then tweak it a bit to look for balls and lines in certain configurations and that would allow me to look for even more specific things i could easily make it look for any shape made up of circles and lines which is a lot of them point is programming is largely about taking large problems that you can t solve and breaking them up into smaller problems that you can solve and in that vein taking solutions you have and extending them or combining them to make them do things that are just a bit more complex do that a bunch of times and you have something that does a very complex task but no individual part of it is very complex']

Name: 55_cfd_deep_wisdom_conventional
Representative_Docs: ['the important factor is whether the model is generalizable i imagine they are hoping to work towards a neural network that can predict the outcome of new cfd calculations after learning on this data set disclaimer i m not in cfd or ml ai but previously did some ml projects my program chair and program in general is very interested in these types of applications i actually saw a colloquium recently by a guy at columbia doing what you guys are talking about his goal however was a more broad functional minimization scheme that took advantage of what we know from ml anyway i share you cynicism in some ways', 'i can t believe i m defending ml in physics but this isn t completely fair obviously it is a fancy curve fit and the speed advantage is there purely because you re solving a curve fit and not the actual physical system but that s not necessarily a bad thing to use a chemistry example because that s what i know if you have a model that truly gets within kcal mol of dft in small molecules that s massive for pharma and all the training required is worth it that s not something i expect to see in the next two decades but that would still be a game changer i don t know if that s a realistic thing in cfd but apparently that has happened in quantum chemistry it s not actually useful because there s not enough computational power to do the training on a sufficiently large bond basis but it worked you gave it a structure and it spit out an answer that is similar quality to dft and while this isn t deep learning i also think there s a lot of potential value in taking ideas from compressed sensing to get real simulation speed up with judicious sampling choices you can get a completely solvable undetermined system which saves you from having to solve for however many points with the real model while this is an ongoing thing graph theory is also proving to be the answer to how do we deal with systematic errors in thermochemistry the data requirements are pretty extreme for that but you create a thermochemical network solve it for self consistency and now you can immediately see whether or not your experiment calculation for a new quantity is sensible it s probably still not perfect but it s a hell of a lot better than the old method which was we have two conflicting values here one was taken by a lab with an h index of and the other by an h index of so i guess we ll go with the data also not coincidentally if a paper uses the words deep learning and it s not explicitly an ml researcher you can pretty safely not read it because it s a trash paper using a model that is ill suited for the system in question now if you ll excuse me i need to take a shower after this post', 'uh lol it can t tell you why it came up with the answer that sentence illustrates your complete naive perception of machine learning first machine learning isn t about getting the answers the paradigm is basically this normal programs take data and rules to provide an answer like a calculator machine learning takes data and answers and tries to provide rules think decision trees next outside of neural nets it s not terribly difficult to determine what s happening in an algorithm regression just a linear function the entire body of regression analysis is about knowing the how and why of regression and executing a set of steps to come up with a statistically supported stable repeatable relationship between features clustering most are just moving centroids around in some fashion and measuring some degree of fitness of the clusters as the data scientist you should know your data subject field and the algorithms well enough to identify meaningful performance measures ahead of time the rest is just selecting an optimization method for drawing boundaries around the data classification very basic splitting strategy and a performance metric probability just math time series just extrapolating additive or multiplicative components of a process series and assessing moments to approximate a range of likely forecast values literally just math again all the other nuanced variations on these common methods are just optimizations for the sake of it or they are very specific to a field this sentiment that machine learning just spits out answers when you give it data is the product of bootcamps and moocs oversimplifying the process and convincing mbas they can be data scientists in three months because they can pump a historic bitcoin price csv through automl this is where so much bad data science comes from and why it is actually valuable to have an advanced degree in the field and formal education in theory the harder are neural nets because the math is extra it s not so much that we don t fundamentally understand them it s that a large network has so many connections explaining what is happening step by step would defeat the purpose of the whole thing because it would take longer than just doing whatever it s doing by hand what should be remembered is that your aren t using the nn for the answers you re training it to build a set of weights that are essentially a set of rules given the architecture in that you have a classifier trial and error is really just experimentation that s proper data science it s not blind trial and error though there is a method and following empirical methodologies leads to results that are repeatable stable and mathematically supported the hype is going to die down because a bunch of hacks have been getting into the field and applying whatever the algorithm flavor of the week is on their mooc and got bad results they didn t know where invalid because they don t actually know what they re doing']

Name: 60_unpaid_internship_unpaid internship_paid
Representative_Docs: ['i think it s up to what you want to do regarding personal project and unpaid internship does the unpaid internship mentor you in a subject you want to do don t take an unpaid internship in qa if you hate qa and want to be a machine learning engineer you can try exploring google summer of code or trying to contribute to openbsd mozilla or some other open source project openbsd and mozilla tend to have roles where mentors are willing to help you and if your google soc proposal is accepted you ll receive a stipend for your work ultimately it depends on your future role if you want to be a front end engineer and you create some networking feature for openbsd it might have been better to create an interesting node or react project as it s more relevant', 'i have a related question i m also at an internship similar to this one since last august on a small family company got basically nothing to do so i kind of find my own tasks like building a website translation of an existing website small chores of the company etc but we have one big difference i don t get paid i never really asked to be paid too i was a rd class statistics student so i was really bad at programming at the start so i volunteered to be an intern from the start we had a mutual interests thing with the company as i would learn from them and they would make me do work so i was a slave basically they decided to cancel a project where i was finally beginning to combine my programming skills with my statistics background so i was really upset btw i wasn t the reason they cancelled it i was all around okay with this place until i heard that some people in my university department were getting paid for an internship where as i wasn t even getting money for food gas etc a couple months later covid lockdown happened and they wanted me to do a data entry job that would take several months i told them i would do it but also told them i was interested in other projects and my education since a data entry job wasn t what i signed up for my boss hasn t answered me still days or so but she didn t like my answer i m sure i m continually progressing myself through machine learning ai data mining related subjects and i would say that i m getting better day by day at what i do as a future data scientist and i also eager to learn should i start looking for other paid internships i live in a developing country and i dream to be working in usa or canada can i look for any remote internships from a first world country if this isn t the place to ask the questions i can open a new topic', 'it really just depends on what you do in the unpaid internship and how much training you receive there s no definitive right or wrong answer if the unpaid internship actually takes the time to develop you and have a clear cut project planned out for you the unpaid internship might be fine or even better than some paid projects in regards to experience but considering they re not paying you it s unlikely they d have a great mentor willing to spend their time to help you out the reality is you ll probably be on your own and won t receive much training even if you manage to complete the project since college students usually don t know how to code in a production environment your project will be missing a lot of best practices during an interview it s common to go over your resume and projects sometimes you even go over the design decisions of your project you may set some red flags off here the argument for your own personal project is that if you are given a project you despise during your unpaid internship might as well work on your own project you want since you aren t getting paid anyways an unpaid internship in the software engineering field you want machine learning security big data etc with proper training and support will be better than a paid internship in a field you don t want in terms of experience currently i d say most internships are remote but previously an unpaid internship could be too high of an opportunity cost as you might have to move somewhere with no financial aid if you believe you will receive great mentoring like the people you re working with and project and have the finances to afford it it might be worth taking that unpaid internship otherwise go with a paid internship you receive or work on your own project contribute to open source if you weren t able to secure a paid internship but received an unpaid internship if you have no other financial costs aside from time i d also recommend you take a look at it if you end up not liking the project you can always just quit on the spot not like they were paying you anyways just don t expect to put it on your resume the unpaid internship might also help with having responsibilities instead of procrastinating on your own personal project during the summer tl dr if you you didn t get a paid internship check it out you can always quit the unpaid internship if you have a paid internship if the unpaid internship has a experience you want great team project lined up for you you can afford to not be paid take it over even a bad paid internship it will probably be better for your resume a bad internship may be an internship you don t like such as one in it or data entry']

Name: 67_resume_resumes_keywords_ats
Representative_Docs: ['gt i was just curious why hr or the people who look at candidates resume before the hiring manager contact the candidates before talking to the hiring manager its part of the prescreening process there is actually a step before hr and that is you have to pass muster with the ats our ai overlords we don t actually see of the applications that are submitted to us i am just speaking about us every company has their own procedures we may get applications for one position but we only look at those resumes that the computer tells us to look at then we phone prescreen of them and weed out everybody who wants an hour for a job that pays and everybody who needs every tuesday off eventually the hiring manager gets half a dozen applications resumes for people that the ats deemed qualified and have been vetted by hr he she then begins the interview process here they choose one primary candidate and two backups today people often renege on job offers move get married or simply get better offers we just call the next candidate with great news rinse and repeat p', 'that ominous evil ai bot that scans resume for keywords probably doesn t exist for better or worse hr isn t moving you forward because recruiters usually don t know the req or don t care they grab resumes with x years of what looks like experience and runs with them rinse and repeat applications and interviews you are the problem not your resume not your leetcode or whiteboard skills it s something about you source was a contract recruiter for years before going back to school to be a dev lol', 'its hard to answer your question without having a specific position in mind generally i have some idea of the skills i am looking for and immediately disregard resumes that don t suggest it primarily through highlights from work experience i spend about seconds looking at a resume for this so it should be quite obvious or it is likely to be missed i am also looking for writing style clarity and sensibility through what you choose to say and not say i ll generally go through the entire batch sent from hr be that or and take out the interesting ones after this step i read personal statements of the selected resumes and am paying attention for writing style and their interests a missing personal statement isn t harmful but isn t helpful to the candidate one that is poorly written is likely to get scrapped one that is poorly aimed will depend on their resume i may call and explore their understanding of the position to see if they may still want to be interviewed a good personal statement can readily go to the top of the pile at this point at this point i have rank ordered applicants and decide who i want to interview in the first round normally people it s important to understand that before any of this happens the majority of applicants are filtered at the hr level hr receives all applications and checks them against the job description the department has their on criteria for receiving an application things they filter include incomplete or untimely applications applicants that do not meet organization department wide criteria these are often regulatory and can include residency status prior employer clearance credentials and such and fit to the job description they use nlp algorithms to filter and then manually review all remaining applicants for mismatches i receive the ones that make it through this hr process they aim to get me x number of applicants so if the job description says ms required and they have lot s of applicants with an ms or higher someone with a bs would have to meet a lot of other required and fit similar work history criteria it does mean that if a bs holder is passed to me it may say something very good about that person on the other hand if not many of the applicants have an ms they will include the best qualified bs graduates to try and reach the target number for the pool hr s review of fit for the job description is highly dependent on hrs and not my interpretation of the job description first most job descriptions are inaccurate and second hr doesn t often fully understand them especially for highly technical fields i ve had expected applications rejected at the hr level because hr didn t know that some of the language in the applicant s resume referred to the same skills and professional activities as what we were looking for but was not standard language for those from training backgrounds that we have historically hired from']

Name: 71_bot_chatbot_discord_chat
Representative_Docs: ['let s imagine i want to create a chat bot that can answer questions natually i guess i would create a neural network with questions and answers however for what i want to do my backend can read and do stuff with i originally thought about simply trying out the damerau levenshtein distance algorithm and then do some comparisons but that s just silly for something like this i am probably going to write the backend in net core if that makes any difference thanks', 'hello for the nlp engine i d recommend dialogflow google s as it is pretty robust and has good documentation although wit ai facebook s just released a new update and it is looking pretty solid from a technical standpoint integrating with this nlp engines is done through webhook integrations which means that you can just create a back end intent handling in any language you want e g python java etc just provision a publicly available https endpoint and register it in your nlp as the fulfillment url for a google dialogflow agent the time to develop a bot depends a lot on what kind of functionality is provided through the bot i e what do you have to do integrate with in your back end how complicated open ended the conversation design and language parameter extraction is which chat bot platforms you are leveraging e g slack facebook messenger etc especially if you are leveraging specific functionality from their infrastructure like account linking group handling for slack channels etc think of the clients slack fb messenger etc as your front end and your back end is split between the nlp engine and your intent handling application sitting behind it as for if this is a good idea i am definitely biased as i am neck deep into this software niche so i would say yes it is an area with a lot of interesting challenges my recommendations would be focus on the conversation design first figure out the conversational paths both good and wrong the user is not as constrained as in a conventional ui so they can easily wander off the intended path only after you have figured out the conversation then start composing your intents the pieces of data to extract in the nlp platform and Name: 73_upload_canvas_zoom_transcript
Representative_Docs: ['one thing that i m not a big fan of is background music maybe during a transition like bumper music but not thru the entire video the other thing is more content less you make it more about the content of the video and less about you if you have you as a pip picture in picture you can have screens that summarize and highlight things an example is an animation explainer video where they draw things on the screen and you just do the voice remember people are there for the content not background music and not to see someone read the voice is a bit mono tone i have the same problem it s not easy to do a voice another things is a forward and a summary aka quick outline of what s going to be talked about the content a summary of the content with important part highlighted remember some people watch these at x speed because there is so much info out there so having a clear visual summary helps to convey the content things like a compare contrast summary table where you highlight items already displayed you can actually do this with a pad of paper and markers or dry erase boards sitting on a holder with the camera fixed on that looks like you re using a mbp so you can use quicktime to connect your iphone and use that as a movable remote camera then make a stand where it points to the whiteboard or pad of paper with the pad of paper you can predraw several screens then just flip from one to the next and highlight with a marker costs maybe for pad and markers get the matt paper that doesn t reflect if you go that route i can t comment on the value of the content as i don t really know aws stuff that well but it should be direct and to the point with the only repeat at the intro and summary here s one example off hand the audio isn t loud and clear and the pad could be the entire focus but you get the idea of how to use a pre made pad of paper you can use automated software for this but i don t have any one that i ve used and these pads can be dirt cheap you can just make a pad out of cheap printer paper and a set of multi color markers i know these things because i m actually doing the same thing for programming ai and economics invested a very small amount of money for a few things to help out i m kinds stuck on the animation software or hand drawn part', 'i have had success with canvas studio which is a licensed derivative of screencast o matic i have also used screencast o matic screencast o matic makes it very simple to record your screen with say your powerpoints and overlay a small picture in picture of your face or record just your webcam or just your screen the free version has some limitations but frankly it s enough to get your lectures down obs is way more complicated and flexible but after learning it i find that it s not usually worth the effort and screencast o matic will do what i need it to without the fuss once i ve recorded i transcribe for captioning using otter dot ai worth every penny edit the transcript on otter download in subtitle format and upload the video and separate transcript file to youtube where i publish the lecture as unlisted the advantages of youtube as a host are that it is rock solid free and students know how to use it and it is very easy to embed in any page canvas or wherever it also allows students to show hide captions speed up or slow down the presentation and show or hide a transcript youtube s analytics are excellent i know how many students have watched a lecture and how far into the lecture most of them checked out if i reformat the subtitle file from srt to webvtt before i upload it the captions will show up in iphones in embedded videos there are a lot of free web pages that will do the conversion it s several steps to get that video published but i think it is worthwhile to make the videos accessible i am not presently disabled but i like watching videos with captions and i think my students learn more readily from them', 'please do it you have no way of knowing if any of your students have hearing issues and it helps even people without hearing problems when the connection is poor it is considered to be an ada best practice it is easy to do depending on your setup in my case i record in zoom and then upload to canvas studio as a normal practice canvas studio gives you a choice of either autogenerating captions or loading a caption file from elsewhere if you use canvas studio to generate the captions the quality isn t all that great but it is ok however i do it a little differently i have my zoom account tied to otter ai which i think is the best auto captioner out there i record in zoom which automatically uploads the file to otter ai which goes to work generating a caption file then after i upload my zoom recording to canvas studio i simply choose the option to upload a caption file from elsewhere and pull in the otter ai captions voila i have a nicely captioned recording']

to actually make it happen here are a number of things you can do yourself to improve your depression symptoms often there is an underlying cause for your depression you might not like yourself or aspects of your life the below advice addresses the symptoms and will reduce them but if you don t address the causes it s not likely to go away for long that s the whole point of therapy with professional guidance figuring out the causes and then addressing them one by one if you want to skip that step you ll have to compensate for it somehow you should review your life and see if there are any obvious causes for your sadness and find ways to remove them or make them more bearable some people will be depressed but don t know why a common reason is that they don t have a purpose to are under if you can t fall sleep try taking melatonin one hour before going to bed it s cheap otc and is scientifically proven to help regulate your sleep pattern also rule out sleep apnea up to of types of meditations for depression relief your attention is like a muscle the more you train it the better the control you have over it mindfulness training will help you gain better control over your mind it doesn t take much effort just to minutes a day of doing nothing but focus your attention is enough and is scientifically proven to work as you become better at focusing your attention it will become easier to force yourself to stop having negative thoughts which will break the negative reinforcement cycle go here if you have specific questions r meditation exercise the effect of exercise on depressions if you have access to a gym then start lifting weights if you don t have access to a gym or you don t like lifting start running if you can t run then start walking just start small minutes three times a week is fine you don t have to run fast just run and then slowly build it up over time exercising does several things it releases endorphins it takes your mind of your negative thoughts and it will improve your overall health give lots of hugs hugs release oxytocin which improves your mood and relaxes you so find people to hug if you are single hug your parents or friends if you can t see if a dog is an option most dogs love to hug another solution that provides the same benefit is a weighted blanket will provide a similar positive effect at night you should to listen to it over and over if you can listen to it hundreds of times it likely has the highest positive effect on your mood the effect can be amplified by using headphones and playing it loud and can further be enhanced by closing your eyes doi doi jmt and doi you are not your depression for some people often those that have been depressed for a long time their depression has become a part of who they are and they assume a victim role but that is a big problem you have to will yourself into someone that sees themselves as a person that is actively fighting their disease that no longer identifies with it or else you will unconsciously obstruct your own healing process as eckhart tolle expressed it in a new earth gt a very common role is the one of victim and the form of attention it seeks is sympathy or pity or others interest in my problems me and my story seeing oneself as a victim is an element in many egoic patterns such as complaining being offended outraged and so on of Name: 78_python_libraries_written_language
Representative_Docs: ['to your actual question it s pretty unlikely anyone in programming will only learn one language in their lifetime so why not start with a language that s easy to learn and it turns out pythons pretty damn powerful and convenient for a lot of uses it seems like if your job involves programming but you don t have programmer in your job description like say you re a researcher or a data scientist you may very well use python for that as for speed speed of execution isn t important until it is and it rarely is example a beat up pickup truck and a high end muscle car are totally different in top speed but if you re driving to work and you spend more time behind other cars waiting for red lights to turn green all that stuff the difference in how soon you get to work isn t really an issue and upgrading that v muscle car to a v won t speed up your commute much either it s very rare that a program isn t fast enough and if it needs to be sped up you find the bottleneck and optimize that part of the code what is really important is how easy it is to write the code and how easy it is to read it because production systems require you to go back and modify old code all the time for bug fixes and new features so it s common to write most of the system in an easy to read language to reduce manpower costs and then if it s too slow rewrite the of the code that is the bottleneck in a faster language this is pretty much how python libraries work in real life machine learning code can create optimized operations that run on a stack of video cards that were built to do that kind of fast math so very efficient but the code to set everything up and run it is in python if you set up a deep learning network once and run iterations to train it making that initial setup a little bit faster is not a significant difference rewriting it in c would be like practicing how to take off your seat belt faster so you could get to work sooner technically it would shave a half second off your thirty minute commute but it ain t worth it', 'gt it uses c libraries to do the work this is exactly what i meant if you were to track for the duration of a ml training run which language the code being executed was written in over of the time it wouldn t be python likely not even in the c based python core libraries this is good if it spent of its time in the python code then we wouldn t be using it and would have replaced it with faster c no the python acts as a wrapper an easy to use api around the efficient c or fortran below the python code isn t doing the work it s passing your parameters to the code that does this distinction doesn t usually matter from an end user perspective so long as the result is good who cares but when you re talking about which language is being used for the actual high performance compute it does matter you re not doing machine learning using python you re controlling a pre compiled machine learning program with python that code that does the actual work is written using cuda most of the time and while yes solutions to use this through python exist like pycuda you find functionality is restricted compared to full fat c cuda for example you cannot write cuda kernels without using c numba is in development but isn t feature complete ie no texture memory dynamic parallelism you can call these kernels using python so you can create an easy to use wrapper but avoiding c when using cuda isn t an option when actually developing these tools aka the job of many a postdoc', 'yes the language matters lower level languages like c c rust pascal fortran and others are compiled directly to machine code and the languages don t really put any arbitrary restrictions on what your code is allowed to do what that means is that if you re clever you can harness the full power of your computer to make your program as fast and as lean as possible higher level languages like java c vb python ruby perl javascript and many others are not pre compiled into machine code but are instead executed inside a runtime environment now some of these may be just in time compiled into machine code but that code is still more limited it works with abstractions that do some of the memory management and computation for you in those languages it s impossible to achieve full speed because the languages abstract too much and never let you go bare metal however these languages usually let you accomplish a lot more with less code so it s a tradeoff note that programs written in higher level languages are not necessarily slow in many cases the bottleneck of a program the part that s the most sensitive to performance is efficient and the rest of the program just doesn t take too long to run so the language doesn t matter one great example of that is tensorflow machine learning is one of the most computationally intensive things you could do on your computer so it might be surprising that machine learning using tensorflow is typically done using python the reason for that is that all of the core parts of tensorflow that do the math and learning are written in c assembly language gpu shader language and other highly optimized languages while the high level stuff like deciding which files to read as input and how many layers go in the neural net are all done in python']

Name: 79_python_language_javascript_learn
Representative_Docs: ['i just came back to learning to code after a couple of years before i stopped i think i pretty much only knew beginner python and tkinter and after the break i ve completely forgotten tkinter instead i decided to re learn beginner python and go a different route so here s what i ve learned so far beginner python intermediate python if finishing this series counts lol kivy made a simple app nothing too advanced matplotlib i m currently trying to decide between options learning a new python library i don t know if there s a library that i need to know there aren t any ones i ve set my eyes on learning ml and ai i m kind of interested in this but i don t know if it s too early or i don t have enough knowledge yet learning a new language i kind of want to learn java or c c i can never tell the difference since i heard they re used a lot in the industry and taught in college i m going to college next year but i don t know which to learn and if i should focus on python more before moving on to a new language i would be interested in hearing the opinions of more experienced people thanks in advance', 'why learn python it s easy af to pick up it abstracts hides away a lot of the nitty gritty aspects of the hardware and gets you real close real fast to the nitty gritty concepts of computer science you can actually do a lot with python webscraping data and machine learning projects apis guis building complete webapps voice and image processing the list is endless python has a very lively and robust community ecosystem so you can find help on the internet readily are there python jobs yes are there a lot depends on where you are python was my first language i m self taught but i m currently working with ruby javascript and go my advice to you is to learn a language not to find a job in that language but to becoming a better programmer especially one who can easily adapt to new technologies additionally once you get comfortable learning python pick up another dynamic language perhaps javascript to learn about high level programming from the perspective of another language once you get comfortable in python and javascript pick up a low level language down the line to fill in the gaps and learn about the hardware my recommendation is go', 'i really don t know why people are disliking python in these comments i think a lot of the comments are kinda wrong let me try and go in depth a bit here first off i do think the first programming language you pick up matters a lot in theory all programming languages can accomplish the same task because they re all turing machines but we don t ever start people off with assembly or something more fun like brainfuck why because morale is a huge part of self learning and expecting people to just power through because hard things are worth it a shockingly common sentiment on reddit is dumb you want to feel like you re making progress and that means getting started quick being able to see things build up in front of you and being able to show stuff off for this reason alone i would drop just about any language that isn t python java or javascript the various c languages are great because they are useful but they are not friendly to set up even before we get into complexity in how they are used i m a swe with a decade of programming experience in cpp and i still hate make files so what about the three mentioned first i want to explain why i think these three pass the filter above all three of these languages are easy to set up and get started with with minimal configuration or tooling javascript is probably the easiest in terms of start up cost you don t even have to install anything just open a browser hit f and boom you have a javascript console where you can do all sorts of things python and java both require installing their respective run times but that s more or less it from here it s worth thinking about what you want to accomplish programming languages are tools and some tools make sense in some situations and make no sense in others you wouldn t pick up a screwdriver to cut a tree probably this is the part where i filter java out for some reason academia loves teaching java to beginners and i think that s really dumb two big reasons first programming in java requires you to use object oriented programming there s nothing wrong with oop but like languages different programming paradigms are also tools oop is a specific tool but to have a language entirely using just oop means you either don t have the ability to do certain things or you re constantly trying to cut down a tree with a screwdriver for a certain subset of problems second programming languages live and die by the libraries that people can create on top of them java has a lot of libraries but they aren t nearly as easy to import and use as they are in python and javascript so for most use cases i think it s faster and more flexible to get started with not java that leaves python and javascript python is imo a great starter language it has so much flexibility and is used in so many real world applications data science machine learning web scraping web serving etc etc python supports object oriented programming and functional programming and procedural programming adding new libraries to your code is generally as simple as import library you have to set up pip for installation which is a small caveat which adds to the quantity of things you can do with the language there are people in the comments who say python is weird compared to other languages and this is kinda true syntactically but picking up syntax becomes second nature very quickly you want to learn core concepts and then extend out and i think python is great for that also again even if python is weird it s one of the most used programming languages out there in terms of quantity it goes javascript python c by a huge margin javascript is a bit weirder than python but if you want to do anything related to the web you re going to want javascript that means web pages multiplayer games anything that you use your web browser for for most use cases js easily plays with html which allows you to incorporate guis from the beginning which for me was really important to feeling like i was doing something useful because most people eventually want to do something with the web it s never a bad idea to get familiar with js hopefully this helps you out feel free to follow up if you have other questions and good luck welcome to programming and all that jazz']

Name: 80_healthcare_medical_ai healthcare_nursing
Representative_Docs: ['i currently work as an er nurse graduated i mainly got into the profession because of family influence and job stability a fall back for med school after going through the program and learning about healthcare i have learned it is not for me i mean this in the sense of workload expectations pay not very good in canada for a new nurse regardless of skillset etc healthcare can be very fulfilling and also regretful at the same time every single senior seasoned staff member i ever spoke to would regret working in the field and wished they did something else but cannot leave because they are too far in and have a family to care for burnout is real and i worry for my work life balance in the future my end goal is to be able to work in a setting that does not put my life at risk earn enough money to buy a house and make a positive difference in the world that helps multiple people at a time and work with like minded individuals my grades and interests in math were better than my science and i have always wanted to work in an office setting for the last year or so i have tried to learn how to program on my own through free courses videos and books i love the idea of creating and manipulating data the way i want it and have it execute the way it s planned unlike telling humans what to do with uncontrollable factors currently i am enrolled in some data science courses to obtain a certificate hoping to be able to have formal learning to add to my resume however i do not know how i could apply it in terms of jobs and real life scenerios i have researched nursing health informatics and their masters programs but there is no clear path for me to follow because it is a growing field is there anyone in a similar situation or can guide me as i am lost i am very open to any guidance or feedback and i would greatly appreciated it i will also answer any questions if my post is not clear enough i apologize if it sounds like a rant i meant for the post to explain my thoughts understanding of my situation tldr i am a registered nurse and i don t see a bright future for the profession in canada especially during this pandemic is there any way i can transition out without having to go back to school for years realistically', 'do you mind explaining what exactly ai in healthcare is or just how is computer science used in healthcare in general if you re thinking about making organs there could be some rather specialized prosthetics that are based on having the person and the machine learn how to interact though that s getting a bit fringe if you re thinking computer programming in healthcare there s much more to be done in medical records and systems that run hospitals epic and cerner are the top names there and even within places like epic you ll find things like compiler specialists writing a typescript to mumps compiler because no sane person wants to actually write mumps but you also get things like computrition btw the jobs page has four jobs that are older than days they re having trouble filling them which manages industrial scale kitchens like the ones in hospitals and has things like making sure that a heart surgery patient has options that are all low in sodium and the app to allow the patient to order a meal from their bed the thing that i want to stress is that most software development isn t the oh neat coding new things but rather the i am working on writing a report about how many potatoes to buy for the french fries for a hospital for next week based on current estimates of bed utilization the highly specialized cutting edge things do exist but that domain of r d isn t what most of us are doing and the professors at the school would have better guidance for how to position yourself for getting those jobs than random strangers on the internet who are mostly other college students and out of work new grads and a sprinkling of grumpy gray beards', 'not in medicine but in another healthcare profession worked with doctors and have doctor friends also have done cs amp x b gt i m mostly interested in health tech and medical devices and i definitely want to one day have my own company i think this is easier to do once you pick up the domain experience in healthcare compared to approaching it from a computer science background when you work in healthcare you get a good sense of what the problems are on top of that you build a strong network a cs education doesn t do this but i noticed that it helps you discover and design solutions to these problems you can get to know what a reasonable solution looks like and how realistic it is many of my colleagues in healthcare don t have this perspective the merits of ai ml in healthcare are classically and routinely misunderstood other notes try to get advice from currently practicing physicians as well when it comes to being a doctor my friends say that you shouldn t do it for the reputation its nice for a bit then wears off quickly reputation is a thing that only parents amp non healthcare friends will care about in healthcare nobody really gives a shit about reputation many of your patients won t give a shit either not for the money either because of the huge debt burden large time cost and relative work life imbalance there are some exceptions but those roles are super competitive if you re going to practice in healthcare you can still have some time alone for family friends life but your life will still be dominated by your dedication to deliver excellent patient care or at least a minimum standard of care that won t get you in trouble it will also be dominated by bureaucracy and politics as well you also have an obligation to keep up to date with advances in your specialization after some time you ll get used to a lot of these issues']
